data.title: Critical Resources launches NZ fieldwork as tungsten market surges
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Critical Resources Ltd ( (AU:CRR) ) has shared an announcement.

Critical Resources has begun ground-based exploration at its Lammerlaw and Croesus projects in New Zealand after securing permit transfer and environmental approvals, targeting structurally controlled gold-antimony and high-grade tungsten systems. Historical work at Croesus has outlined high-grade tungsten mineralisation and a 5 km gold-antimony trend with past high-grade gold production and rock chip samples, while Lammerlaw fieldwork focuses on priority targets with historical gold and tungsten anomalies consistent with an orogenic gold system.

The company highlights its large, low-cost New Zealand landholding in two of the country’s most prospective gold and critical minerals regions, supported by government fast-track reforms and a sharply higher tungsten price environment driven by Chinese export restrictions and supply security concerns. Critical Resources also confirmed completion of its January 2026 capital raising of $1.75 million, including a significant commitment from its chairman and managing director, underpinning ongoing exploration activity and signalling confidence in the New Zealand growth strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:CRR) stock is a Hold with a A$0.01 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Critical Resources Ltd stock, see the AU:CRR Stock Forecast page.

More about Critical Resources Ltd

Critical Resources Limited is an Australian-listed exploration company focused on gold, antimony and tungsten projects, with a 1,694 km² portfolio in New Zealand’s Otago and Reefton regions. The company targets structurally controlled orogenic gold and high-grade tungsten systems, positioning itself to benefit from rising demand and supply concerns for critical minerals such as tungsten.

YTD Price Performance: -9.09%

Average Trading Volume: 2,718,301

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$25.55M

Hearts and Minds Investments Ltd ( (AU:HM1) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Hearts and Minds Investments Ltd has updated its notification to the market regarding an upcoming dividend on its ordinary fully paid shares, relating to the six‑month period ended 31 December 2025. The company confirmed that this announcement is an amendment to its 18 February 2026 notice, specifying details linked to its dividend reinvestment pricing and key dates including the ex‑date of 25 February and record date of 26 February 2026.

By clarifying the dividend terms and confirming it remains tied to the December 2025 half‑year period, Hearts and Minds provides greater certainty for shareholders planning their income and participation in the reinvestment plan. The update reinforces the company’s ongoing capital management approach, which is important for investors who rely on consistent distributions and seek to compound returns through reinvested dividends.

PEXA Group Limited has issued a total of 56,650 unquoted performance rights under its employee incentive scheme, with allocations made on 10, 12 and 17 March 2026. data.title: LaFleur Minerals Chairman Lifts Stake via Mining Claims Option Deal
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

LaFleur Minerals ( (TSE:LFLR) ) has issued an update.

LaFleur Minerals has issued an early warning report after Bullrun Capital, a private venture firm controlled by LaFleur chairman and director Kulwant (Kal) Malhi, received 4 million LaFleur shares. The shares were issued under an option agreement granting LaFleur an exclusive right to acquire a 100% interest in certain mining claims and a mining lease held by Bullrun.

Following the transaction, Malhi’s direct and indirect stake in LaFleur rose from 8.85% to 12.61% on a non-diluted basis, and from 11.75% to 15.28% on a partially diluted basis, modestly increasing insider ownership and influence. Bullrun said it acquired the shares for investment purposes and may adjust its position over time, signaling potential future shifts in LaFleur’s ownership structure depending on market conditions and strategic opportunities.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:LFLR) stock is a Sell with a C$0.48 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on LaFleur Minerals stock, see the TSE:LFLR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on LFLR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LFLR is a Neutral.

The score is primarily weighed down by pre-revenue operations with accelerating losses and significant ongoing cash burn, which dominate financial performance risk. Offsetting factors include a strengthened balance sheet with positive equity and low leverage, while technical indicators point to weak-to-neutral momentum and valuation metrics are not supportive due to negative earnings and no dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on LFLR stock, click here.

More about LaFleur Minerals

LaFleur Minerals Inc., listed on the CSE, OTCQB and Frankfurt exchanges, is a Vancouver-based mining company focused on acquiring and developing mineral assets. The company advances its portfolio through option agreements on mining claims and leases, using equity-based consideration to secure and consolidate interests in targeted resource properties.

Waseda Academy Co., Ltd. ( (JP:4718) ) has shared an announcement.

Waseda Academy has announced a change in one of its major shareholders based on the shareholder register as of March 31, 2026, received from Mitsubishi UFJ Trust and Banking Corporation. Individual investor Shinichi Kawabata, based in Shibuya, Tokyo, increased his stake from 1,850,000 shares to 1,941,500 shares, raising his voting rights ratio from 9.97% to 10.46% while remaining the second-largest shareholder.

The revision in holdings reflects a modest consolidation of ownership but does not alter the company’s ranking of major shareholders or its governance structure as disclosed. Waseda Academy stated that this shift in shareholding will have no impact on its business performance, suggesting limited immediate implications for operations or strategy, though it may be noted by investors tracking ownership concentration and shareholder influence.

Waseda Academy Co., Ltd. is a Japanese education services company listed on the TSE Prime market, operating preparatory schools and related academic programs. It focuses on offering entrance exam preparation and supplementary education primarily for students in Japan, competing in the highly competitive private education and cram school sector.

The latest announcement is out from BeijingWest International ( (HK:2339) ).

BeijingWest Industries International Limited has requested a halt in trading of its shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange effective from the morning of 8 April 2026. The suspension comes ahead of an upcoming announcement containing inside information and is linked to matters governed by the Hong Kong Code on Takeovers and Mergers, signaling that a potential corporate transaction or change of control may be under consideration.

The decision to halt trading suggests that the company is managing the disclosure of potentially price-sensitive information to ensure an orderly market, which could have significant implications for shareholders and potential bidders. Investors are likely to closely monitor the forthcoming disclosure, as any takeover-related development could reshape the company’s ownership structure, strategic direction, and valuation in the automotive components sector.

data.title: WT Financial names new CFO to drive advice-sector consolidation push
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from WT Financial Group Ltd. ( (AU:WTL) ).

WT Financial Group has appointed Michael Peters as its new Chief Financial Officer in a move aimed at strengthening execution of its consolidation and growth strategy in the financial advice sector. Peters, who brings more than 30 years’ experience in banking, financial services and listed markets, will also lead the existing finance function and is expected to assume the role of company secretary, bolstering governance and ASX reporting.

The appointment is closely aligned with WT Financial’s push to scale its Investco joint venture and Hubco model, providing equity capital to high-potential advice practices and driving both organic and acquisitive earnings growth. Management emphasised that Peters’ deep M&A, capital structuring and advice-network relationships should materially enhance the group’s capacity to execute on a strong pipeline of consolidation opportunities and create long-term value for shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:WTL) stock is a Hold with a A$0.14 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on WT Financial Group Ltd. stock, see the AU:WTL Stock Forecast page.

More about WT Financial Group Ltd.

WT Financial Group Limited is one of Australia’s largest financial adviser networks, offering wealth management, retirement planning and personal risk insurance advice. Its services are primarily delivered through about 400 privately owned practices operating under brands including Wealth Today, Sentry Advice, Synchron Advice and Millennium3, alongside a B2C arm under Vesta Wealth Partners.

Through its joint venture WTL & MWP Investco with Merchant Wealth Partners, the company provides long-term, non-controlling capital to support corporatisation and consolidation of advice practices. WT Financial also runs a central Wealth Adviser division that supplies support, training and extensive financial literacy resources to advisers and clients within and beyond its network.

data.title: BSP Financial Group Names Michael Makap to Board
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

BSP Financial Group Ltd. ( (AU:BFL) ) just unveiled an announcement.

BSP Financial Group Limited has appointed Michael Makap as a director, effective 8 April 2026, filling a casual vacancy created by the resignation of Stephen Beach in March. The board, led by chairman Robert Bradshaw, has formally welcomed Makap and expects him to strengthen governance with his broad business background.

Makap brings extensive executive and boardroom experience from roles including CEO of Motor Vehicle Insurance Limited, advisor to ExxonMobil’s PNG LNG project, and director positions at Pacific MMI, Pacific Re Insurance and Pacific International Hospital. His appointment underscores BSP’s strategy of leveraging local and regional leadership expertise to support its expanding regional banking franchise and complex stakeholder base in the South Pacific.

More about BSP Financial Group Ltd.

BSP Financial Group Limited is the leading bank in the South Pacific, with roots in Papua New Guinea dating back to 1916. It serves around 3 million retail, business, corporate and institutional customers across Papua New Guinea, the Cook Islands, Fiji, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Tonga and Vanuatu through 124 branches, 596 ATMs and a growing suite of digital banking services.

Jackpot Digital ( (TSE:JJ) ) has issued an update.

Jackpot Digital Inc., a provider of dealerless electronic poker table games to casinos globally, is focused on delivering efficient, revenue-generating alternatives to traditional live-dealer tables. The company’s products target operators seeking to modernize their floors with innovative electronic table games that streamline costs and improve player engagement.

The company plans to raise up to C$2.1 million through a convertible debenture financing with 10% annual interest and attached share purchase warrants. Proceeds are earmarked for regulatory licensing fees, product certifications in multiple jurisdictions, and general working capital, a move that could support broader market expansion but remains subject to stock exchange approval and includes anticipated insider participation.

data.title: Meta Capital Exits Substantial Holding in Solution Dynamics via Director Transfer
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Solution Dynamics Limited ( (NZ:SDL) ).

Meta Capital Limited has ceased to be a substantial shareholder in Solution Dynamics after transferring its entire holding via an off-market related party transaction. The transfer involved 1,600,658 ordinary shares at $0.55 per share, moving Meta Capital’s stake from 10.09% of issued capital to zero.

The shares were transferred to John Stephen McMahon, who is both the sole director and 100% owner of Meta Capital and a director of Solution Dynamics. As a result, the disclosure serves both as a substantial holding notice and as a director’s interest disclosure, clarifying McMahon’s direct ownership position for investors and regulators.

More about Solution Dynamics Limited

Solution Dynamics Limited is a New Zealand-listed company, though the release does not specify its exact industry or core services. The company is subject to New Zealand’s Financial Markets Conduct Act disclosure rules, reflecting its status as a listed issuer with obligations around substantial shareholding and director interests.
